entries.title: Validator Operations
entries.summary: Reference material for validator uptime, identity, and maintenance.

entries.title: Xahau Buildout
entries.summary: Working notes for future Xahau marketplace and AI integrations.

entries.title: Trading Fundamentals: Markets & Order Types
entries.summary: Core concepts for understanding how markets operate, order types, execution basics, and how these apply across crypto and traditional markets.
details: Describe market participants: retail, professional traders, market makers, exchanges/venues and liquidity providers.
Explain order types: market, limit, stop (stop-loss, stop-limit), IOC/FOK, and conditional orders and their execution risk.
Differentiate venue types: CEX order-book exchanges vs DEX AMMs and implications for slippage and MEV.
Outline execution considerations: latency, slippage, partial fills, order routing and best execution practices.
Summarize position management basics: leverage, margin, maintenance margin and liquidation mechanics (especially on crypto margin products).

links.label: Binance - Order Types & How Orders Work

entries.title: Technical Indicators: EMA, RSI, Fibonacci and Common Tools
entries.summary: Reference guide for widely used indicators (EMA, RSI, Fibonacci retracements) including interpretation, common settings, and pitfalls.
details: EMA: definition, calculation differences vs SMA, common periods (9, 12, 21, 50, 200), use in trend-following and cross strategies.
RSI: oscillator range (0–100), overbought/oversold rules (70/30 heuristic), divergence, and smoothing with EMA.
Fibonacci retracement: key ratios (23.6, 38.2, 50, 61.8), how to draw retracements from swing high/low, use as confluence zones with support/resistance.
Combine indicators conservatively: avoid indicator stacking without price/market-structure confirmation; prefer indicator + price action + liquidity context.
Watch for look-ahead and survivorship bias when backtesting indicator strategies.

entries.title: Liquidity & Market Structure
entries.summary: Operational framing of liquidity concepts, order book dynamics, support/resistance, supply/demand zones, and how to read market structure.
details: Define liquidity metrics: bid/ask spread, depth, volume at price levels, market impact and slippage.
Order book dynamics: understand depth, resting orders, cancellations, iceberg orders and how order flow changes price.
Market structure primitives: higher highs / higher lows (uptrend) vs lower lows / lower highs (downtrend), structure breaks as trigger signals.
Liquidity zones: cluster significant limit order levels, previous high-volume nodes, and on-chain token holder concentration for crypto.
Use combined data: time & sales (tape), order book snapshots and VWAP to assess short-term execution risk.

links.label: TradingView - Volume Profile & Market Structure tools

entries.title: Market Makers, Whales & Flow Analysis
entries.summary: How market makers and large holders ('whales') affect price discovery, liquidity provision, and manipulative patterns in crypto markets.
details: Role of market makers: provide two-sided quotes, reduce spreads, may have inventory and risk limits — understand incentives and times when they withdraw liquidity.
Detect whale flows: large transfers between wallets/exchanges and on-chain jumps can presage price moves or liquidity drains.
Watch for manipulation vectors: spoofing, wash trading, concentrated sell-offs or wash bids on low-liquidity pairs (common in some spot/alt markets).
On-chain whale tracking: monitor large transfers to exchanges, exchange inflows/outflows and known wallet labels for probability of sell-side pressure.
Combine on-chain signals with order book and exchange flow for higher-confidence hypotheses about imminent moves.

links.label: Whale Alert - large crypto transfer tracker
Nansen - Wallet labeling and flow analytics

entries.title: Admin Project: XRPL Trading Bot
entries.summary: Owner-only monitoring dashboard and server adapter skeleton to observe XRPL wallet, Xahau tokens, Flare F-assets and EVM sidechain balances. Includes UI assets and a small server-side xrpl.js adapter skeleton. No secrets are stored in the repo.
details: Files added under admin-projects/xrpl-trading-bot/: index.html (UI), client.js (read-only client), style.css (red/black theme), README.md (developer notes).

Server adapter skeleton added at lib/xrpl/client.js which provides init(), getStatus() and a poll-based account_tx helper. Install and configure xrpl.js in server environment; secrets (XRPL_WALLET_SEED, RIPPLED_URL) must be injected at runtime via a secrets manager.

UI expects owner-only endpoints: GET /api/admin/trading/status, GET /api/admin/trading/txs?limit=, GET /api/admin/trading/metrics. Server must preserve existing owner auth and whitelist when implementing these routes.
No signing or wallet seed entry in the UI. All sensitive operations and seeds are explicitly out-of-repo and out-of-browser per guardrails.

entries.title: Connected XRPL Wallet: rVeHp61gJ9MxoqMkAMmEbow8KYWk3AH1X
entries.summary: Record that the owner indicated an XRPL address to be used with the admin XRPL trading bot dashboard. Seed is not stored in repo; operator must configure XRPL_WALLET_SEED in environment or secrets manager at runtime.
details: Address: rVeHp61gJ9MxoqMkAMmEbow8KYWk3AH1X (public address only).
DO NOT store the wallet seed in the repository. The server-side XRPL adapter reads XRPL_WALLET_SEED and RIPPLED_URL at runtime from environment variables.

Dashboard: admin-projects/xrpl-trading-bot shows connection status and balance by calling GET /api/admin/trading/status. Owner authentication required via OWNER_WALLETS and x-owner-wallet header in this skeleton implementation.
If you want to operate the bot, set XRPL_WALLET_SEED in your secret manager and configure OWNER_WALLETS (comma-separated) to include the owner address(es).

entries.title: XRPL Trading Strategies Catalog
entries.summary: A curated, owner-only catalog of XRPL-native trading ideas (RSI mean-reversion, spread capture, LP, whale-flow hedges, periodic rebalance) including triggers, sizing, prerequisites and operational notes.
details: RSI mean-reversion strategy between XRP and issued rUSD with trustline prerequisite and conservative sizing.
Spread capture for Xahau tokens and small arbitrage windows; emphasizes small order size and depth checks.
Liquidity provision primitives with inventory skew management and hourly rebalance.
Whale flow reactive hedging to reduce downside risk on large inbound/outbound flows to gateways/exchanges.
Scheduled periodic rebalance for baskets of tokens to capture drift while limiting churn.

entries.title: Agent Brain: XRPL Trading Operations
entries.summary: Notes and decision heuristics for an owner-only automated agent that watches XRPL state and suggests or (with owner permission) executes trades. Focus on observability, safe sizing, trustlines and avoidance of on-repo secrets.
details: Operate in two modes: observe-only (alerts + simulation) and gated execution (server-side with secret-managed seed and owner whitelist).
Require explicit trustline checks before any issued-token exposure. UI must never request seeds; server-side adapter does signing only when secrets are present at runtime.
Maintain conservative sizing defaults and per-strategy stop-loss / inventory caps. Prioritize auditable logs for every decision and action.
Prefer server-side order books, gateway liquidity checks and on-chain tx confirmation steps for actual execution. Client UI remains for watch/sim only.

entries.title: XRPL Strategy Runner: sma-small-01
entries.summary: Initial strategy runner workspace created: a small SMA crossover example that polls a simulated orderbook, logs trades, and exposes an owner-only dashboard. Simulated trades by default; real execution enabled only when XRPL_WALLET_SEED and RIPPLED_URL are provided at runtime.
details: Workspace: admin-projects/workspaces/xrpl-strategy-bot/ contains a self-contained app (server, public UI, adapter skeleton, JSON DB).

Strategy: simple SMA crossover on mid-price with very small default trade size (TRADE_SIZE env var). Enter on short>long crossover, exit on opposite. Trades simulated unless secrets are present. All trades persisted to local db/db.json for auditing.
Owner access: endpoints require x-owner-wallet header to match OWNER_WALLETS. Do not put secrets into the repo; use runtime secret manager for XRPL_WALLET_SEED and RIPPLED_URL.
Next steps (automated): monitor metrics/trades, iterate on strategy parameters, add additional strategies to the runner loop and maintain per-strategy state.

entries.title: Personal XRPL Trading Bot (owner-only)
entries.summary: Owner-only personal trading bot UI and XRPL adapter skeleton for monitoring Xahau, XRPL, Flare and an EVM sidechain. Red/black themed dashboard with owner-gated API endpoints and a local DB for metrics/trades. No secrets in-repo; XRPL seed must be injected at runtime.
details: Workspace created at admin-projects/workspaces/personal-xrpl-bot/ with a self-contained server, UI, and XRPL adapter.
UI: index.html, client.js, style.css — red/black theme, visually oriented to watch XRPL, Xahau, Flare and EVM-sidechain metrics.

Server: server.js exposes owner-protected endpoints: GET /api/admin/personal-bot/status, GET /api/admin/personal-bot/metrics, POST /api/admin/personal-bot/poll, POST /api/admin/personal-bot/simulate-trade.
XRPL adapter: lib/xrpl/client.js with init() and getStatus() using xrpl.js; will attempt connection only when RIPPLED_URL (and optionally XRPL_WALLET_SEED) are provided at runtime.
Security: Owner auth enforced by x-owner-wallet header matching OWNER_WALLETS env var. DO NOT put XRPL_WALLET_SEED in repo; use secret manager at runtime.
Local DB: db/db.json stores metrics and simulated trades for audit and review.

entries.title: Project: Personal XRPL Trading Bot — Workspace Added
entries.summary: Added an owner-only Personal XRPL Trading Bot workspace under admin-projects/workspaces/personal-xrpl-bot. Includes UI, owner-guarded Express server, XRPL adapter skeleton using xrpl.js, and initial DB for metrics and simulated trades. A link to the workspace was added to the projects listing.
details: Workspace files: index.html, client.js, style.css, server.js, lib/xrpl/client.js, db/db.json, package.json, .env.example, README.md, cbot-project.json.

XRPL adapter: lib/xrpl/client.js provides init(), getStatus(), and pollSample() using xrpl.js. It performs read-only probes by default. XRPL_WALLET_SEED is optional and must be provided via secure runtime secrets to enable signing (not implemented in this skeleton).

Owner auth: server enforces x-owner-wallet header against OWNER_WALLETS environment variable for all API endpoints. If OWNER_WALLETS is not set, the endpoints allow access but the server includes a warning — owners should set OWNER_WALLETS in runtime configuration.
APIs: GET /api/admin/personal-bot/status, GET /api/admin/personal-bot/metrics, POST /api/admin/personal-bot/poll, POST /api/admin/personal-bot/simulate-trade.
UI: simple dashboard showing status, latest metrics, simulated trades and ecosystem token placeholders for Xahau, Flare and an EVM sidechain. Buttons are themed red & black.
Security: DO NOT commit XRPL_WALLET_SEED or other secrets. Use .env or secret manager at runtime.
Preview: Project is accessible via the owner-only project hub route: /admin/projects/workspaces/personal-xrpl-bot/ when the workspace server is run for preview.
Projects index updated: admin-projects/projects/index.html now includes a card & link to the personal-xrpl-bot workspace for owner preview.
XRPL adapter: lib/xrpl/client.js provides init(), getStatus() and pollSample() using xrpl.js. It operates in simulated mode if RIPPLED_URL is not set at runtime.

Owner auth: server enforces x-owner-wallet header against OWNER_WALLETS environment variable. If OWNER_WALLETS is not set, the server logs a warning and allows access (owners should set OWNER_WALLETS in runtime configuration).
APIs: GET /api/admin/personal-bot/status, GET /api/admin/personal-bot/metrics, GET /api/admin/personal-bot/trades, POST /api/admin/personal-bot/poll, POST /api/admin/personal-bot/simulate-trade.
UI: red & black themed dashboard showing status, metrics and simulated trades. Client includes an owner wallet input to set the x-owner-wallet header for previewing owner-only endpoints.
Security: DO NOT commit XRPL_WALLET_SEED or other secrets. Use .env or a secret manager at runtime.
